---
title: "PAPER TITLE"
description: "One sentence summary for the article listing."
author: "Santosh Sawant"
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
categories:
  - llm
  - research-paper
paper-title: "PAPER TITLE"
paper-org: "ORG NAME"
paper-url: "https://arxiv.org/abs/XXXX.XXXXX"
paper-date: "Month YYYY"
image: 01.jpeg
toc: true
reading-time: true
---

<div class="tag-badges">
  <span class="badge-tag badge-llm">LLM</span>
  <span class="badge-tag badge-paper">Research paper</span>
</div>

<div class="post-meta">
  <span>📅 DATE</span>
  <span>🏢 ORG</span>
</div>

<div class="paper-card">
  <div class="paper-icon">📄</div>
  <div>
    <div class="paper-title">PAPER TITLE</div>
    <div class="paper-meta">ORG · arXiv XXXX.XXXXX · DATE</div>
  </div>
  <a class="paper-link" href="ARXIV_URL" target="_blank">Read paper ↗</a>
</div>

![Architecture figure caption](01.jpeg){.post-cover}

::: {.callout-tip title="Key Innovation"}
What is genuinely new here — one or two sentences.
:::

BODY TEXT — explain the paper in plain language. Aim for 3-5 paragraphs.
Each paragraph = one key idea. No walls of text.

::: {.mermaid-wrapper}
```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Step 1] --> B[Step 2] --> C[Step 3]
```
:::

CONTINUE EXPLANATION if needed...

::: {.callout-note title="Key Result"}
The single most important finding or benchmark number.
:::
